companies.challenges: 【NeoCloud】【Technical Barriers】i3D.net has extremely high requirements for low latency, high performance, and high reliability, with infrastructure covering six continents, making technical integration and maintenance complex. Existing suppliers like Dell and Supermicro have established stable supply chains; ASUS must prove significant advantages in performance, stability, and global service support. Especially, game hosting demands strict CPU single-core performance, GPU computing, and network latency. 【Supply Chain Barriers】As a Ubisoft subsidiary, procurement decisions may involve group-level approvals and strict requirements on global service capabilities and rapid response. 【Business Challenges】Gaming and media industries are cost-sensitive; ASUS must provide competitive TCO solutions and demonstrate global service support capabilities. 【Industry Characteristics】Gaming workloads are highly volatile, requiring suppliers to provide flexible scaling solutions and rapid delivery capabilities.
companies.entryPoint: 【NeoCloud】【Short-term (6-12 months)】Highlight ASUS server advantages in high performance, low latency, high density, and cooling technologies, especially optimized for game hosting and AI/ML workloads. Provide detailed technical white papers, test reports, and success cases. 【Mid-term (1-2 years)】Establish deep cooperation with i3D.net technical team, provide customized server solutions, and offer rapid response technical support and maintenance through ASUS global service network. 【Long-term (2-3 years)】Become i3D.net's core supplier in global gaming and AI infrastructure fields, providing global supply chain and service support as business expands. 【Key Actions】Prioritize contact with Giovanni Intini's technical team via direct supply channel and provide game server-specific performance benchmark reports.

companies.challenges: 【neocloud】【Technical Threshold】SAP Cloud Infrastructure is based on OpenStack and Kubernetes, and relies heavily on the Intel platform, with strict requirements for hardware compatibility, stability, and performance. Existing suppliers are all large cloud service providers or well-known hardware manufacturers. ASUS needs to prove the compatibility and advantages of its products in the SAP ecosystem. 【Supply Chain Barriers】SAP Cloud Infrastructure is mainly based on OEM procurement and has established cooperative relationships with multiple hyperscale cloud service providers. ASUS needs to find differentiated competitive advantages and prove that its supply chain capabilities can meet large-scale, customized needs. 【Business Challenges】ASUS's brand awareness in the enterprise server market may not be as good as existing suppliers, and more resources need to be invested to build trust. 【Industry Characteristics】As the world's largest enterprise software company, SAP's cloud infrastructure procurement decisions involve multiple layers of approval and have strict requirements for suppliers' enterprise-level support capabilities and long-term commitments.
companies.entryPoint: 【neocloud】【Short-term (6-12 months)】Targeting the AI workload requirements of SAP Cloud Infrastructure, promote ASUS's GPU server solutions, emphasizing their advantages in AI computing performance, cooling technology, and energy efficiency, and strive for small-scale pilot projects. At the same time, actively participate in SAP's hardware certification program to ensure full compatibility of ASUS servers with SAP HANA and OpenStack/Kubernetes environments. 【Mid-term (1-2 years)】After successfully completing the AI pilot project, gradually expand to general-purpose Intel servers, providing competitive cost-performance and customization options. It is possible to indirectly enter the supply chain of SAP Cloud Infrastructure by cooperating with SAP's existing hyperscale cloud service providers as one of their hardware suppliers. 【Long-term (2-3 years)】Establish a long-term cooperative relationship to become a strategic hardware supplier for SAP Cloud Infrastructure, providing a full range of server products and solutions, and participating in the planning and design of its future infrastructure. 【Key Actions】Prioritize obtaining SAP HANA hardware certification and establish an official cooperative relationship through the SAP partner program.

companies.challenges: 【neocloud】【Technical Threshold】Policloud focuses on distributed, sovereign cloud solutions, emphasizing high-density GPU computing, edge deployment, and low environmental impact. Its micro data center design is unique and has strict requirements for server customization, cooling, energy efficiency, and size. ASUS needs to prove that its server products can be seamlessly integrated into Policloud's modular containers and meet its requirements for water cooling, low power consumption, and high computing density. 【Supply Chain Barriers】The software integration of Policloud with Hivenet also needs to be considered. ASUS needs to ensure that its hardware is compatible with Hivenet's software platform. 【Business Challenges】Policloud's goal is to challenge hyperscale cloud providers, and its competitors may include CoreWeave, NVIDIA, and VAST Data. ASUS needs to demonstrate the advantages of its products in performance, cost-effectiveness, and customization. 【Industry Characteristics】The French distributed cloud market has extremely high requirements for data sovereignty and localized services, and needs to comply with specific French environmental and energy regulations.
companies.entryPoint: 【neocloud】【Short-term (6-12 months)】Targeting Policloud's micro data center design, provide customized solutions for ASUS high-density GPU servers (such as ESC8000-E12), emphasizing their advantages in cooling, energy efficiency, and size, and provide POC testing.
